packageRules.description: Loki and Mimir validation imports drive the operator's validation behavior. E2e suite must pass before merge.

github.com/grafana/mimir was forked from Cortex in 2022 and started its own versioning at v2.0.0. The pre-fork v1.x tags still exist in the repo but declare their module path as github.com/cortexproject/cortex, so Go module resolution refuses to import them as github.com/grafana/mimir. Constrain Renovate to true Mimir versions only — PR #8 (closed) demonstrated this when Renovate proposed v1.10.0.

gopkg.in/yaml.v2 and gopkg.in/yaml.v3 are SEPARATE Go modules under semver-import-versioning — the vN is part of the module path, not just a version tag. The project deliberately imports both: yaml.v2 because Loki's upstream Limits API uses it; yaml.v3 (and go.yaml.in/yaml/v3) for Mimir's. Renovate misinterprets v2 → v3 as a major version bump for the same module and produces an invalid go.mod (PR #10, closed). gopkg.in/yaml.v2 is in maintenance mode and unlikely to see further releases anyway.

These are upstream-fork pins in go.mod `replace` directives that coordinate with the Loki and Mimir bumps. `go mod tidy` refreshes them as a side effect of bumping grafana/loki/v3 or grafana/mimir — Renovate should not propose them independently. PR #2 (closed) demonstrated this: Renovate proposed a digest where the fork had already moved to v4, breaking the v3 replace.
Actions are SHA-pinned with a `# vX.Y.Z` tag comment. Renovate updates both the SHA and the comment together (helpers:pinGitHubActionDigests preset + pinDigests:true).
